predictions.text: IEA member countries will publicly report (IEA statement, member government releases, or IEA Monthly Oil Market Report) that cumulative crude/product releases executed and/or firmly scheduled for delivery total at least 300 million barrels under the March 2026 coordinated release plan.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves TRUE if the IEA or a compiled set of official member announcements explicitly sums to ≥300,000,000 barrels executed and/or scheduled for delivery under the coordinated March 2026 action by 2026-05-31. Otherwise FALSE.
evidence: IEA agreed to a record 400M-barrel release; U.S. planned 172M barrels from the SPR (CNBC, 2026-03-11): https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/11/iea-oil-reserves-crude-prices-iran-g7-energy.html
monitoring_signals: IEA press releases and statements on emergency stockdraws
IEA Monthly Oil Market Report references to coordinated releases
U.S. DOE/SPR tender awards and delivery schedules
Official energy ministry announcements from IEA members
predictions.rationale: A coordinated release is already agreed; the key uncertainty is execution/scheduling at scale. A 300M threshold tests follow-through without requiring full 400M completion by May.

predictions.text: ICE Brent front-month futures will settle at or above $130.00 per barrel on at least one trading day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves TRUE if the official ICE Brent front-month settlement price is ≥$130.00/bbl on any trading day from 2026-03-12 through 2026-06-30. Otherwise FALSE.
evidence: Crude prices rose despite the IEA agreeing to a record release, reflecting elevated supply-risk fears (CNBC, 2026-03-11): https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/11/iea-oil-reserves-crude-prices-iran-g7-energy.html

Equity futures fell sharply as oil climbed and tail risk was repriced (MarketWatch, 2026-03-11): https://www.marketwatch.com/story/dow-futures-down-nearly-500-points-as-oil-prices-continue-to-climb-9affa0b8?mod=mw_rss_topstories
monitoring_signals: ICE Brent settlements (front-month) daily
News on Strait of Hormuz transit risk and outages
OPEC+ communications and any emergency policy shifts
IEA/SPR release pace vs. disruption headlines
predictions.rationale: Geopolitical disruption risk can overwhelm releases, but $130 requires substantial escalation and/or sustained outages; hence moderate-low probability.

predictions.text: EIA will report U.S. regular gasoline retail prices (weekly, all formulations) at or above $4.25 per gallon for at least one week.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves TRUE if the U.S. EIA Weekly Retail Gasoline and Diesel Prices report shows 'Regular All Formulations' at ≥$4.25/gal for any publication dated on or before 2026-06-30. Otherwise FALSE.
evidence: Oil price spike and shipping risk in/near the Strait of Hormuz increases downstream fuel price pass-through risk (CNBC, 2026-03-11): https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/11/cargo-ship-struck-strait-of-hormuz-uk-iran-war.html
Crude prices rose on supply-risk fears despite planned releases (CNBC, 2026-03-11): https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/11/iea-oil-reserves-crude-prices-iran-g7-energy.html
monitoring_signals: EIA weekly retail gasoline series
RBOB gasoline futures and crack spreads
U.S. refinery utilization and outage reports
State-level price spikes (AAA) indicating national lift
predictions.rationale: Retail gasoline can lag crude; reaching $4.25 typically needs sustained crude strength and/or refinery constraints.

predictions.text: UKMTO and/or the International Maritime Bureau (IMB) will report at least 10 distinct security incidents affecting commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz or Gulf of Oman within any rolling 30-day window.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves TRUE if UKMTO advisories/incident logs and/or IMB piracy/incident reports document ≥10 separate incidents (attacks, boardings, missile/drone strikes, or confirmed security events) in the Strait of Hormuz or Gulf of Oman during any consecutive 30-day period ending by 2026-04-30. Otherwise FALSE.
evidence: Multiple cargo ships were struck near Iran, including in the Strait of Hormuz (CNBC, 2026-03-11): https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/11/cargo-ship-struck-strait-of-hormuz-uk-iran-war.html
monitoring_signals: UKMTO advisories and incident reports
IMB Piracy Reporting Centre updates
AIS-based reporting of disruptions/deviations
Insurance market bulletins referencing incident frequency
predictions.rationale: Incident tempo is already elevated; a 10-in-30-days threshold is aggressive but plausible if strikes and harassment continue.

predictions.text: Drewry World Container Index (WCI) will print at or above $5,000 per 40-foot container (FEU) in at least one weekly release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves TRUE if any Drewry WCI weekly publication dated on or before 2026-05-31 reports the composite index ≥$5,000/FEU. Otherwise FALSE.
evidence: Shipping disruption risk is rising around Iran/Hormuz, increasing freight and rerouting pressures (CNBC, 2026-03-11): https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/11/cargo-ship-struck-strait-of-hormuz-uk-iran-war.html
monitoring_signals: Drewry WCI weekly releases
Freightos Baltic Index as a cross-check
Carrier general rate increase (GRI) announcements
Rerouting/port congestion indicators (AIS, port wait times)
predictions.rationale: WCI can spike quickly under severe disruption, but reaching $5k requires broad capacity dislocation beyond a localized security flare-up.

predictions.text: The Federal Reserve (Board or FOMC) will announce a new or expanded liquidity backstop explicitly citing market functioning or funding stress (e.g., expanded standing swap lines terms, new repo operations, or a temporary facility) in an official press release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves TRUE if a Federal Reserve press release, FOMC statement, or FRB New York statement by 2026-05-31 announces a new liquidity facility or a material expansion (size/terms/counterparties/frequency) of an existing backstop and explicitly references market functioning, funding stress, or similar. Otherwise FALSE.
evidence: Options markets pricing elevated tail risk and equity futures falling sharply amid oil spike suggests potential for funding/vol stress transmission (MarketWatch, 2026-03-11): https://www.marketwatch.com/story/dow-futures-down-nearly-500-points-as-oil-prices-continue-to-climb-9affa0b8?mod=mw_rss_topstories
monitoring_signals: FRB and FRBNY press releases on operations/facilities
SOFR-OIS spread and Treasury market depth metrics
FX basis and cross-currency funding stress indicators
Primary dealer survey mentions of market functioning
predictions.rationale: The bar for new facilities is high; this triggers mainly if volatility impairs funding/market functioning rather than just risk-off.

predictions.text: The S&P 500 (SPX) will close down by at least 5.0% in a single trading day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves TRUE if SPX official close-to-close daily return is ≤ -5.0% on any trading day from 2026-03-12 through 2026-06-30. Otherwise FALSE.
evidence: Equity futures fell sharply as oil climbed, with tail-risk repricing tied to Iran conflict escalation (MarketWatch, 2026-03-11): https://www.marketwatch.com/story/dow-futures-down-nearly-500-points-as-oil-prices-continue-to-climb-9affa0b8?mod=mw_rss_topstories
monitoring_signals: VIX term structure and SPX skew
Intraday oil spikes and conflict headlines
Credit spreads (CDX IG/HY) and liquidity conditions
Large-cap earnings guidance revisions tied to energy/shipping
predictions.rationale: A -5% day is uncommon but becomes more plausible under abrupt escalation, disorderly energy move, or sudden policy shock.

predictions.text: USTR will publish a Federal Register notice under Section 301 proposing new or increased tariffs of at least 10% on goods from at least one of: Mexico or the European Union, covering at least $50 billion in annual import value (as stated in the notice or accompanying release).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves TRUE if by 2026-06-30 USTR publishes a Section 301 Federal Register notice proposing additional tariffs meeting: (i) rate ≥10% (headline rate), (ii) applies to Mexico and/or EU-origin goods, and (iii) the notice or official USTR release states coverage of ≥$50B annual import value. Otherwise FALSE.
evidence: Trump administration launched Section 301 trade probes into major partners including Mexico and the EU (CNBC, 2026-03-11): https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/11/trump-trade-investigations-ieepa-tariffs.html
monitoring_signals: USTR Federal Register notices and press releases
USITC/USTR docket postings and hearing schedules
Trade press reporting on proposed tariff lists and coverage totals
White House proclamations or fact sheets referencing Section 301
predictions.rationale: Investigations create a pathway to tariffs, but large, fast implementation against Mexico/EU depends on political calculus and negotiation dynamics.

predictions.text: At least one U.S. Big Tech firm (Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, or Apple) will disclose in an SEC filing that it expects incremental 2026 capex to increase by at least $10 billion (company-specific) versus its prior guidance, explicitly citing AI infrastructure or data-center build-out.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves TRUE if an SEC filing (10-Q/10-K/8-K) by any listed firm states a revised 2026 capex plan/guidance ≥$10B higher than its previously communicated guidance (as referenced in the filing) and the filing explicitly attributes the increase to AI infrastructure and/or data centers. Otherwise FALSE.
evidence: Tech firms are reallocating and investing around AI; e.g., Atlassian cutting ~10% to self-fund AI and enterprise investments (CNBC, 2026-03-11): https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/11/atlassian-slashes-10percent-of-workforce-to-self-fund-investments-in-ai.html
Google closed acquisition of Wiz, indicating continued scaling in cloud/security offerings (Wiz blog, 2026-03-11): https://www.wiz.io/blog/google-closes-deal-to-acquire-wiz
monitoring_signals: 10-Q/10-K capex disclosures and earnings call transcripts
Data-center lease announcements and power procurement deals
Supplier capex commentary (server, networking, cooling)
AI training/inference demand indicators (GPU orders, utilization)
predictions.rationale: AI build-outs remain capex-intensive; a ≥$10B upward revision is sizable but plausible if demand/competition accelerates or supply constraints ease.

predictions.text: Oracle will disclose in an earnings release or 10-Q/10-K that remaining performance obligations (RPO) are at least $150 billion.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves TRUE if Oracle, in any earnings release or SEC filing dated on or before 2026-12-31, reports Remaining Performance Obligations (or equivalent contracted backlog metric explicitly labeled RPO) ≥$150B. Otherwise FALSE.
evidence: Oracle shares jumped on strong results easing AI build-out concerns (CNBC, 2026-03-11): https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/11/oracle-orcl-stock-q3-earnings-ai-data-center.html
monitoring_signals: Oracle quarterly earnings releases and 10-Q/10-K filings
Commentary on cloud bookings/backlog and large AI contracts
Capex guidance for OCI expansion
Enterprise AI infrastructure demand indicators
predictions.rationale: If OCI demand and large multi-year AI contracts accelerate, backlog can compound; $150B is a high bar requiring sustained mega-deals.

predictions.text: A Phase 3 clinical trial for an oral pharmacologic treatment for obstructive sleep apnea will be registered on ClinicalTrials.gov, with planned enrollment of at least 500 participants.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves TRUE if ClinicalTrials.gov posts a new study record by 2026-12-31 labeled Phase 3 for an oral drug targeting obstructive sleep apnea with anticipated enrollment ≥500. Otherwise FALSE.
evidence: Scientists reported a potential pill for sleep apnea, suggesting pipeline momentum toward larger trials (ScienceDaily, 2026-03-11): https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/03/260311004816.htm
monitoring_signals: ClinicalTrials.gov new registrations and updates for OSA oral drugs
Company press releases on Phase 3 initiations
FDA meeting/fast-track/breakthrough announcements for OSA drugs
Key opinion leader conference abstracts (ATS, SLEEP)
predictions.rationale: Many promising early results fail to scale; requiring a large (≥500) Phase 3 trial keeps this anchored to meaningful development commitment.
